nodes.name: Cache Eviction Policy
nodes.description: Changed from LRU to LFU in deployment v4.2.1

nodes.name: Cache Hit Rate
nodes.description: Percentage of requests served from cache

nodes.name: Database Query Count
nodes.description: Number of queries reaching the database per second

nodes.name: Server Memory Limit
nodes.description: Reduced from 16 GB to 12 GB in same deployment v4.2.1

edges.mechanism: LFU evicts recently-added but infrequent keys; for bursty workloads this reduces hit rate vs LRU
Each cache miss results in exactly one additional database query
Each additional DB query adds ~40 ms median; under load, connection pool contention amplifies tail latency
Smaller memory limit reduces cache capacity, directly increasing eviction rate regardless of policy
Reduced memory triggers OS-level swapping under high load, adding I/O wait to request processing

mechanisms.description: Eviction policy change reduces cache hit rate, which increases DB query volume, which raises p99 latency through connection pool contention
Memory limit reduction raises latency through two independent paths: reduced cache capacity and OS swapping

confounders.description: Deployment v4.2.1 changed both eviction policy and memory limits simultaneously. Without controlling for memory, naive correlation overstates the causal effect of the eviction policy alone.

interventions.action: Revert eviction policy to LRU while keeping memory limit at 12 GB

expectedEffects.expectedChange: Hit rate improves 10–15% for bursty workload patterns
p99 latency decreases 20–30% from policy-driven contribution; memory-driven contribution remains
